key_insight: MISTnetX uses deep learning to transfer atomistic shock–microstructure physics from molecular dynamics into continuum finite-element models, enabling prediction of shock-to-detonation across scales without explicit scale separation.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical path for parameter-free simulation of energetic materials, improving predictive design and safety assessment of nanostructured explosives.
method: Trained a convolutional neural network on large-scale MD simulations of shock propagation through complex microstructures.
Captured shock–microstructure interactions, hotspot formation, and transition to deflagration as sub-grid inputs to FE models.
Coupled mechanics, shock, thermal transport, and chemistry in a continuum framework for run-to-detonation prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to a synthetic but realistic nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composite, where the model enabled full run-to-detonation prediction from MD-informed FE simulation.
watch_item: The demonstrated case is synthetic and specific to one energetic-material system, so broader transferability and robustness across microstructures and chemistries still need validation.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
